why: Canonical kebab-case reason: static capability mismatch — retry to max_deliver, then dead-letter.
Canonical: sovereignty/policy refusal — capable but declines; retry-then-dead-letter.
Canonical: transient load — exponential backoff, excluded from the max_deliver exhaustion budget.
Canonical: M7 attestation refusal — immediate dead-letter fast path, never retried.

DRIFT/COLLISION: cortex and myelin's own specs/admission.md emit snake_case; a parser keyed on the canonical kebab set drops it. Guards transport/nak-vocab-cross-repo-drift — a myelin kebab dead-letter failure is classified null/'high' instead of 'critical' by cortex's snake_case projection. Retirement is OPEN DECISION OD-1.

cortex added a fifth value (policy_denied) consumer-side with no myelin registry entry; it is not in the closed canonical set. Guards the append-without-negotiation drift; disposition is OPEN DECISION OD-1.
Missing reason. RFC §3.4 RECOMMENDS a receiver treat unknown/missing as cant-do; that fallback is the orphaned f-022 rule, currently unimplemented — the vector pins the intended disposition.
Canonical 8-4-4-4-12 lowercase UUID — the form crypto.randomUUID() emits.
MASKING: emit is lowercase but the deployed UUID_RE carries /i, so uppercase validates. Two spellings of one id both pass; emitters MUST lowercase on emit (RFC §8.1).
The grammar constrains neither version nor variant bits, so the nil UUID passes. A finding recorded in Registry §; a workflow keyed on a nil correlation_id joins unrelated envelopes.
A non-UUID correlation_id is rejected.
Concrete, wildcard-free mailbox — the form executeRequestReply mints and the guard accepts.
Subject-injection guard: a '>' would subscribe the responder's reply onto a wildcard, capturing traffic beyond the mailbox. Guards request-reply reply_to injection.
A '*' token wildcard is rejected by the guard for the same injection reason.
Bare '_INBOX.' with no id is rejected — a reply published there would fan out to every inbox subscriber.
A reply_to that does not start with '_INBOX.' is rejected — prevents redirecting replies onto the durable tasks tree.
Legacy 5-segment form: prefix+principal preserved, the subcapability 'typescript' dropped, the capability preserved, 'dead-letter' inserted.

Stack-aware 6-segment form preserves the stack segment. NOTE: this — the spec's PRIMARY form — does NOT match the TASKS_DEAD stream filter local.*.tasks.dead-letter.> (the * binds 'acme', then literal 'tasks' fails against 'default'), so the 30-day audit retention silently does not apply. Guards transport/dead-letter-stream-misses-stack-aware-subjects; OPEN DECISION OD-4.
The federated prefix mirrors the local grammar identically.
Idempotency: re-deriving an already-dead-letter subject is a no-op, so re-routing an already-escalated task cannot double-nest the reserved segment.
A subject with no 'tasks' segment has no dead-letter derivation; the deriver throws rather than fabricate one.

MASKING: 'dead-letter' PASSES CAPABILITY_TAG_RE and is minted today (taskSubject('acme','dead-letter') builds a work subject inside the escalation tree). The spec REQUIRES rejection at publish time; this vector currently FAILS against myelin — the reservation is held by no guard. Guards transport/reserved-dead-letter-segment-unenforced. Co-owned with RFC-0002.
not-now initial backoff delay in ms; delivery=1 is the first nak attempt.
Deterministic doubling from JetStream deliveryCount: 2^(3-1)*1000 — stateless, survives consumer restarts.

Backoff caps at 60s. Rows 4-7 of the curve are unreachable under the reference consumer max_deliver:3, because JetStream counts every delivery regardless of nak reason — OPEN DECISION OD-3 (not-now silent task loss).
deliveryCount is clamped to >=1, so a caller passing 0 sees the initial delay rather than a 2^-1 underflow.
compliance-block routes to dead-letter immediately (fast path) at any chain length — the same M7 policy would refuse on every peer, so retrying only burns budget.
cant-do/wont-do accumulate; when the chain length (excluding not-now) reaches maxDeliver (default 3, aligned to the TASKS consumer) the task routes via exhaustion.

not-now NEVER routes to dead-letter — it is excluded from the exhaustion budget regardless of chain length (F-022 contract). This is precisely why OD-3's silent-loss can occur at the JetStream layer below.
Below the exhaustion threshold no dead-letter route fires; the task is redelivered.
